description: EBCDIC ordering: 'a' > '1' is FALSE (opposite of ASCII). EBCDIC: a=0x81, 1=0xF1.
notes: In EBCDIC cp037: 'a'=0x81, '1'=0xF1. 0x81 < 0xF1, so 'a' < '1' and IF WS-A > WS-B is false → WS-RESULT='N'. In ASCII 'a'=0x61 > '1'=0x31, so ASCII would give 'Y'. Tests ebcdic_compare() for lowercase-vs-digit ordering.
